title: Why was Claude Mythos really delayed? OpenAI points to Anthropic's massive compute constraints, says report
summary: OpenAI has claimed that it has a significant advantage over Anthropic due to its early expansion of computing resources. The company also said in a leaked memo that Claude Mythos was delayed due to computing issues.

title: 'The biggest consumer fraud': Telegram CEO claims WhatsApp is secretly reading your private messages
summary: Telegram's CEO Pavel Durov has criticized WhatsApp's encryption claims, labeling them as ‘biggest consumer fraud in history’. Durov asserted that WhatsApp reads users' messages and shares them, a sentiment echoed by Elon Musk.&nbsp;

title: OpenAI takes on Anthropic, overhauls ChatGPT Pro subscription with new AI plan: here's what you need to know
summary: OpenAI has launched a $100/month ChatGPT subscription, offering increased Codex usage to compete with Anthropic's Claude Code. The new Pro tier provides access to exclusive features and the company is also offering a temporary promotion for existing users.

title: Elon Musk says WhatsApp's end-to-end encryption can't be trusted, Mark Zuckerberg's company calls it ‘absurd’
summary: Elon Musk criticized WhatsApp, saying 'can't trust' it, in light of a class-action lawsuit alleging Meta intercepted private messages despite WhatsApp's end-to-end encryption promises. He also encouraged users to switch to X Chat, which he promotes as an encrypted chat experience.

title: Indias 5G consumers will reach over a billion by 2030: Jyotiraditya M Scindia
summary: Union Minister of Communications, Jyotiraditya M Scindia, stated that India will have more than a billion users of 5G by the year 2030. Speaking at AIMA's 11th National Leadership Conclave, the Minister highlighted that India became the second-largest mobile phone producer globally.

title: Mint Explainer | Can AI find bugs humans can’t? Inside Anthropic’s Project Glasswing
summary: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing aims to use advanced AI to detect hard-to-find software vulnerabilities. Backed by Big Tech, the initiative may reshape how software is secured and could eventually disrupt cybersecurity firms and IT services models.

title: Appeals court rebuffs Anthropic in latest round of its AI battle with the Trump administration
summary: Appeals court rebuffs Anthropic in latest round of its AI battle with the Trump administration

title: Meta unveils Muse Spark AI to rival Google, OpenAI; stock rallies 7%
summary: Meta also released Contemplating mode, which runs multiple AI agents in parallel to boost reasoning power, allowing Muse Spark to take on the extended thinking modes of Google's Gemini Deep Think and OpenAI's GPT Pro.

title: How to use Google Gemini's new AI shopping features in India: Check step-by-step guide
summary: Google has introduced new AI shopping features in India across the Gemini app, AI Mode in Search, and Circle to Search. Users can shop more efficiently with seamless transitions, product comparisons, and easy identification of items across platforms.

title: Keep your number hidden: WhatsApp reportedly begins rolling out username feature to change how you chat
summary: WhatsApp is rolling out a new username feature that allows users to communicate without sharing phone numbers, according to a new report. Currently the feature is only available to a limited number of users

title: Can’t trust WhatsApp: Elon Musk, Pavel Durov warn against platform’s alleged privacy failures
summary: Elon Musk and Pavel Durov have criticised WhatsApp over privacy concerns. Musk said "can't trust WhatsApp" and instead promoted X Chat, while Durov called it the “biggest consumer fraud in history”. Their remarks follow a US lawsuit questioning WhatsApp’s privacy practices.

title: TSMC's Q1 revenue jumps 35% YoY, beats market forecasts
summary: TSMC will ⁠report full first-quarter earnings on April 16, including ​an updated outlook for the current quarter ​and the full year. The company, whose customers include Nvidia, has been a major beneficiary of advances in AI, which has more than offset a tapering-off in pandemic-led demand for chips

title: SpaceX posted nearly $5 billion loss in 2025: The Information
summary: SpaceX reported a nearly $5 billion loss in 2025 despite strong revenue, partly due to its acquisition of xAI. The company remains profitable overall and is pursuing an IPO, aiming for a valuation above $1.75 trillion while advancing space travel and AI ambitions.

title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law
summary: The company says the law would force ⁠it to ‌alter its flagship AI model, Grok, to reflect the ​state's views ​on diversity and discrimination rather than being objective. xAI, which recently merged with SpaceX, is seeking a court declaration that the law is unconstitutional and an injunction blocki

title: Chinese startup ShengShu raises $293 million to advance artificial general intelligence
summary: Founded in early 2023 by ​Tsinghua University alum Zhu Jun, ShengShu became the first Chinese company to release a video generation model when it launched Vidu in April 2024. The model was positioned as a competitor to OpenAI's Sora, which the US company later discontinued.

title: Former DeepMind researchers bet on visual AI with new startup
summary: Andrew Dai has launched Elorian, a startup aiming to improve AI’s ability to understand and reason about visual information. Backed by major investors, the company is developing specialized models to support real-world applications like robotics and design, addressing limitations in current AI syste

title: Meta pulls ads aimed at recruiting plaintiffs for social media addiction lawsuits
summary: At the end of March, ‌a Los Angeles jury ⁠found Meta ⁠and Alphabet's Google liable for a young woman's depression and suicidal thoughts after she said she became addicted to Instagram and Google's YouTube at a young age, ordering them to pay ​a combined $6 million in damages.

title: OpenAI 'Stargate' executive Peter Hoeschele exits: Report
summary: OpenAI 'Stargate' executive Peter Hoeschele exits, per a report by The Information on Friday.

title: Meta transfers top engineers into new AI tooling team
summary: The reorganisation ⁠comes as Meta plans sweeping layoffs that could eliminate tens of ​thousands of jobs at the company, as it seeks to offset costly ​artificial intelligence infrastructure bets and prepare for greater efficiency brought about by AI-assisted workers.

title: Bessent, Powell warned bank CEOs about Anthropic model risks
summary: The meeting, held at the Treasury Department ​in Washington on Tuesday, was aimed at ⁠ensuring banks ‌are aware of the potential risks posed ​by Mythos ​and similar models, and are taking steps ⁠to defend their systems. Access to Mythos will be limited to about 40 technology companies, including Mic
